name: Green Chard and Slivered Carrots
recipeInstructions:
This can be either a hot side dish or a room-temperature salad.
WARM THE OLIVE OIL in a pan with the garlic and chile peppers. Once the garlic slices are brown, remove them and set the oil and peppers aside. Separate the Swiss chard stems from the leaves. Cut the leaves into large pieces and wash them. Trim the uneven ends off the stems. Peel off the thin, transparent outer skin from each stem by catching the edge of a knife under the top layer and pulling it down the length of the stem. If needed, first cut the stem in half lengthwise before peeling both sides. Cut the peeled stems into 2-inch lengths, then into narrow strips. Peel the carrots and cut them into long, 1/4-inch diagonal slices, then into narrow strips. Bring 4-to-5 quarts of water to a boil and add 4 teaspoons of salt. Cook the chard stems and carrots separately until tender (about 1 minute for carrots, 2-to-3 minutes for chard stems). Remove them from the water and set aside. Plunge the Swiss chard leaves into the boiling water and cook until tender (3-to-5 minutes depending on quality). When done, remove the leaves to a colander, press with the back of a wooden spoon to remove excess water, then combine with the stems, carrots, and toss with olive oil. Season with salt and serve with vinegar or lemon wedges.
